mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the effective federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to expected corporate cash flows, which increases present equity values and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Federal funds rate declines fail to produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in a majority of months, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points within a three-month window signals deteriorating labor income and household demand, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes show positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of instances, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer and residential demand, which reduces aggregate revenue expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint month-over-month declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 20-day returns with Rank IC above 0.03, or positive returns occur in more than 55 percent of joint-decline months.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 250, a positive month-over-month change in the index signals elevated inflation that erodes real corporate earnings and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 250, positive month-over-month changes do not produce significantly negative 5-day returns compared to baseline, or t-test p-value exceeds 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative decline in industrial production exceeding 1.5 percent over two months signals a deepening supply-side contraction, which compresses corporate profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45 percent of cases, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects rising aggregate economic output and lower recession probability, which lifts corporate revenue expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth fails to correlate with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns with Rank IC above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in the unemployment rate and decreases in payroll employment indicate a broad and accelerating labor market deterioration that reduces household income expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between the UNRATE-PAYEMS interaction and 5-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 5-day returns occur in more than 50 percent of interaction months.

mechanism_narrative: In tight labor markets where the unemployment rate is below 4 percent, month-over-month declines in industrial production signal binding capacity constraints and rising unit labor costs that compress profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 4 percent and INDPRO declines month-over-month, positive 60-day S&P 500 returns occur in more than 50 percent of instances, or Rank IC falls below 0.03.
